headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one underlined word and one gap. From the list of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	The **handsome** profit from her fish business made Mrs. Uba stop complaining about her husband’s.....................
2.	Chief Osa prefers **fame** in the village to ................ in the city
3.	The media houses should employ both the **novice** and the .................. journalist.
4.	While the Registrar considers the matter **insignificant**, his deputy sees it as ......................
5.	It pays to be **firm** rather than .................. in a case like this
6.	Usually, Joseph is **articulate** in his speech, but today he sounds ..................
7.	The man’s first wife is **loyal** while the second is ..................
8.	Rather than **convict him**, the impartial judge ................... him.
9.	Instead of reconciling her parents. Roxanne worked hard at ............... them.
qs: 10.	We thought that Uncle Sam’s health had **improved** but it had rather ...................

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences.

S2.qs: 11.	One very important aspect of human relationship is .............trust.
12.	The two tasks were performed .................... and we were able to finish early.
13.	The committee is charged with finding ................. a solution to the crisis.
14	We are worried by the ..................... that exists between the two factions of the party
15	Our teacher was not speaking ................... when he said that the bursar was under a cloud.
16.	The signature on the document lacked ..................; so it was rejected.
17.	Everyone could tell that Binta was happy from the ................ on her face
18.	His last novel was ............... by his visit to the prisons.
19.	As a result of the power cut, production at the paint factory came to a	....................
20.	Several ........................ were passed at the meeting.

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 21.	James and Henry were at daggers drawn when I knew them. This means that they

opts.q0: A. were always angry with each other
opts.q2: C. were good at drawing daggers

S3.qs: 22.	My book has become dog-eared through use. This means that

opts.q0: A. the corners of the pages of my book, are crumpled
opts.q1: B. my book is designed like dog’s ears

24.	Whenever my cousin comes to realize that I am telling the truth, she will eat humble pie. This means that my cousin will
25.	I had dinner with tony and Nick and they talked shop the whole time. This means that they talked

opts.q0: A. about buying things from the shop
opts.q3: D. about taking me to the shop

S3.qs: 26.	His decision to reconcile with his former agent paid off. This means that the reconciliation

opts.q1: B. cost him a lot of money

S3.qs: 27.	The beauty of Bimpe’s garden shows that she has green fingers. This means that Bimpe

opts.q0: A. always paints her finger green
opts.q1: B. is good at growing plants
opts.q2: C. turns everything she touches to green

S3.qs: 28.	I would have visited you but for my father's ill health. This means that my

opts.q0: A. father’s ill health would have made me to visit you
opts.q1: B. father’s ill health prevented me from visiting you
opts.q2: C. father threatened to fall ill if I visited you
opts.q3: D. father did not fall ill, so I couldn’t visit you

S3.qs: 29.	Her husband’s death has forced her to tighten her belt. This means that she has
30.	Some candidates take examinations in their stride. This means that they

opts.q1: B. do not worry about them

S4.headd: From the words or group of words letterred. A to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word or group of words as it is used in the sentences.

S4.qs: 31.	The provocation had **an instantaneous** effect on him
33.	If he **declines** the offer, it will be better for him
35.	Such utterances are **inimical** to the peaceful co-existence of the two communities
36.	The chairman observed that Andrew was the most **qualified** candidate for the job
37.	What do you intend to do with these **inanimate** objects?
38.	Everybody noticed that the chairman's excuse was **lame**.
39.	The president appointed **staunch** supporters of his policies as advisers
40.	The little boy gave his candid opinion of the incident.

S5.headd: From the word or group of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

S5.qs: 41.	Why are you always jealous .............. other people?
42.	It is sad that the rich .............. pity the poor.
43.	The motorcycle nearly hit Jane and ............

45.	It is about time you ........... to bed

49.	The meeting of the ............ will come later today
51.	The teachers don’t teach us ............. ?
52.	We are no ............. friends
53.	Can you tell us how the accident came .......... ?
54.	She wondered if the water was not ................ hot for drinking
55.	The robbers were subjected ............... a thorough beating by the irate mob.
56.	The cook should have .................. the pepper and the tomatoes together
57.	My friends would not believe me if I .................. them this story
58.	Even before his burial, Okoye’s family members were already planning how to share his wealth ............. themselves
59.	The principal advised all the students to be of help to	.........................

opts.q0: A. will not treat us like that
opts.q1: B. would not treat us like that
opts.q2: C. will not have treated us like that
opts.q3: D. would not have treated us like that

S5.qs: 64.	The teacher made us .................. five poems each
65.	Each one of the ten chosen contestants ....................... lunch with the patron
66.	The teacher needs ................... to write the note on the chalkboard
67.	Madam Abike turned ................. 13 bags of maize at the first harvest.
68.	The teacher, as well as his students ...................... working very hard
69.	My mother remarked that it was high time we ..................... preparing the supper
70.	All we need .............. dedicated teachers
71.	Though Yusuf is a much younger lawyer, you cannot compare his eloquence ...................... his master’s.
72.	The student wondered why the sun constantly rises ................... the east.
73.	Mrs. Kofi is angry ................... her daughter’s lack of interest in her business.
74.	Until I heard him myself, I .................... imagined that he said that.
75.	No sooner had the war started .................. the soldiers deserted the village.
